spec_match.rationale: The page now names and demonstrates the affected surfaces: the removed xml-stylesheet SVG-producing path, DOMParser.parseFromString(markup, "image/svg+xml"), the XSLTProcessor JavaScript bridge, inline SVG via createElementNS, and the unaffected standalone .xsl boundary for non-SVG or non-MathML output.

interactive.rationale: chrome-devtools-mcp exercised the Parse and render SVG button; DOMParser returned an SVGSVGElement, the preview rendered, and the XSLTProcessor bridge produced an SVGElement.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The compatibility matrix, removed-path explanation, live DOMParser round-trip, and migration pattern all align with the concept's stated compatibility-lab purpose.

edge_cases.rationale: The page distinguishes the removed inline XSLT-to-SVG path from supported DOMParser, inline SVG, server-side rendering, and non-SVG standalone XSLT output paths.

references.rationale: The see also section now links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5143784390262784, MDN XSLTProcessor, MDN DOMParser.parseFromString(), and the feature conformance probe.

design_system.rationale: Raw SVG color literals were replaced with design tokens; chrome-devtools-mcp confirmed no #hex, royalblue, or fill="white" matches remain in the rendered document.

summary: Open questions resolved. Before the fix, chrome-devtools-mcp confirmed the page lacked a ChromeStatus link, still contained raw SVG color literals, and did not surface the non-SVG XSLT boundary. After the fix, the page exposes the ChromeStatus reference, removes raw color literals, names the removed xml-stylesheet SVG-producing path, exercises DOMParser and XSLTProcessor through the live control, and keeps network requests successful. Chrome reports the expected XSLT deprecation warning when the bridge call intentionally exercises XSLTProcessor; no JavaScript errors were observed.
